action.reasoning: Observation: best full-test exploration_score=0.6065 (from iter14, success~0.89 on proxy but full-test around 0.6057 with success_rate~0.873, travel~338m; rooms weakest). Recent iters stuck at proxy-only eval (9 maps, ~0.85 proxy score) due to predict.py truncation + map loading that defaults to maps_train subset (no maps_eval in local /data). Analysis: the dijkstra-based global (utility**beta)/(dist**alpha+1) target selection + next-hop neighbor stepping is a strong, standard, 0-param frontier-based planner fully compatible with real cogniplan_env.ExplorationEnv, provided node utilities/graph, and eval contract. Root cause of proxy-only = incomplete/truncated run_evaluation (cut mid-string) + fragile config/glob that doesn't reliably hit maps_eval in harness. New alpha=2.0/beta=1.5 (higher beta emphasizes large-utility frontiers more, should help room success while controlling dist). Decision: overwrite with FULL compact non-truncated predict.py (kept proven select_waypoint, added robust map loading: prefer eval_config.json list, then maps_eval/**/*.png if present for full 150, else proxy 3-per-type from maps_train; full per_episode collection, strict per_type init for all 3 keys, success-only travel_distance, exact JSON contract, correct exploration_score=(1/N)*sum(S_i*300/d_i), per-ep try/except, always write output). Update train.py with new params+v22 desc. Run train (saves model) + local proxy predict for validation (~0.86 expected), then submit_eval on checkpoint to trigger official full 150-map hidden test score.
action.command: python train.py && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline --output output.json
